rulings.Case Title: EDWARD LOPEZ et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 17, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALFRED PISTILLI et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 17, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Elliott Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is continued to November 30, 2010 at 9:30am to be heard after the hearing on plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the deposition of defendant¿s PMK/COR. However, the court has determined that, notwithstanding the absence of California case law on point, a premises owner who has asbestos on the premises has a duty under California law to act with reasonable care toward a ¿take-home bystander.¿ = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LYNN PATRICK SCHMIDT VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 17, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Dana Companies, LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from satisfying its burden of production that plaintiff does not have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he handled asbestos-containing Victor gaskets. Paragraph 8 of the declaration of Mr. Cohen¿s declaration creates a triable issue regarding plaintiff¿s exposure to asbestos fibers from the Victor gasket. Because there is no evidence that there is a material difference between automobile and boat gaskets on the issue of release of asbestos fibers from the handling of gaskets, the objection to paragraph 8 of Mr. Cohen¿s declaration is overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW CAESAR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 17, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant FMC Corp. on behalf of its former Peerless Pump business¿ motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 and 2 (negligence and strict liability) and granted as to issues 3-5 (false representation, premises owner/contractor liability and punitive damages). Plaintiff¿s declaration creates a triable fact whether he was exposed to packing attributable to defendant in the pump he worked on near Highway 180. The 2007 deposition of Mr. Link creates a triable issue whether that packing contained asbestos. The objections to plaintiff¿s declaration and the 2007 deposition of Mr. Link are overruled. Plaintiff concedes the lack of merit to his claims for false representation, premises owner/contractor liability and punitive damages. = (220/HEK)
